University of Greenfield  
Student Handbook & Administrative Policies (2025)  
 
1. Academic Calendar  
• Semester Start Date:  September 9, 2025  
• Mid-Semester Break:  October 21 –25, 2025  
• Final Exams:  December 5 –15, 2025  
• Semester End Date:  December 20, 2025  
 
2. Attendance Policy  
Students are expected to attend at least 75% of scheduled classes  for eac h course.  
• Absences due to illness require a medical certificate  
• More than 25% absence  may result in:  
o Ineligibility for final examinations  
o Academic probation  
• Attendance is recorded weekly by instructors  
 
3. Course Registration  
• Course registration opens two weeks before semester start  
• Students must register via the Student Portal  
• Late registration is permitted for 5 working days  
• Course changes (add/drop) are allowed within the first two weeks  
 
4. Assessment & Grading  
• Final grades are composed of:  
o Coursework: 40%  
o Midterm exam: 20%  
o Final exam: 40%  
• A minimum score of 50%  is required to pass a cours e 
• Grade appeals must be submitted within 7 days  of result release  
 
5. Academic Integrity Policy  
The university maintains a zero-tolerance policy  toward academic misconduct.  
Academic misconduct includes:  
• Plagiarism  
• Cheatin g during examinations  
• Unauthorized use of AI tools without permission  
Penalties may include:  
• Grade reduction  
• Course failure  
• Suspension or expulsion for repeated offenses  
 
6. Student Support Services  
Students have access to:  
• Academic advising  
• Counseling services  
• Disability support services  
• Career development workshops  
Appointments can be booked through the Student Services Office  or online  portal.  
 
7. Office Hours & Contact Information  
Student Services Office  
• Monday–Friday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM  
• Email: studentservices@greenfield.edu  
• Phone: +1 (555) 123 -4567  
 
8. Graduation Re quirements  
To be eligible for graduation, students must:  
• Complete all required courses  
• Achieve a minimum CGPA of 2.0  
• Settle all outstanding fees  
• Submit graduation application by the announced deadline  
 
9. Complaints & Ap peals  
Students may submit complaints related to:  
• Academic assessment  
• Administrative decisions  
• Student services  
All complaints must be submitted in writing  and will be reviewed within 10 working days .

summaries:
**University of Greenfield Student Handbook & Administrative Policies (2025)**

**I. Academic Calendar**

* Semester Start Date: September 9, 2025
* Mid-Semester Break: October 21 –25, 2025
* Final Exams: December 5 –15, 2025
* Semester End Date: December 20, 2025

**II. Attendance Policy**

* Students are expected to attend at least 75% of scheduled classes for each course.
* Absences due to illness require a medical certificate.
* More than 25% absence may result in:
  - Ineligibility for final examinations
  - Academic probation
* Attendance is recorded weekly by instructors.

**III. Course Registration**

* Course registration opens two weeks before semester start.
* Students must register via the Student Portal.
* Late registration is permitted for 5 working days.
* Course changes (add/drop) are allowed within the first two weeks.

**IV. Assessment & Grading**

* Final grades are composed of:
  - Coursework: 40%
  - Midterm exam: 20%
  - Final exam: 40%
* A minimum score of 50% is required to pass a course.
* Grade appeals must be submitted within 7 days of result release.

**V. Academic Integrity Policy**

* The university maintains a zero-tolerance policy toward academic misconduct.
* Academic misconduct includes:
  - Plagiarism
  - Cheating during examinations
  - Unauthorized use of AI tools without permission
* Penalties may include:
  - Grade reduction
  - Course failure
  - Suspension or expulsion for repeated offenses.

**VI. Student Support Services**

* Students have access to:
  - Academic advising
  - Counseling services
  - Disability support services
  - Career development workshops
* Appointments can be booked through the Student Services Office or online portal.

**VII. Office Hours & Contact Information**

* Student Services Office:
  - Monday–Friday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM
  - Email: studentservices@greenfield.edu
  - Phone: +1 (555) 123 -4567

**VIII. Graduation Requirements**

* To be eligible for graduation, students must:
  - Complete all required courses
  - Achieve a minimum CGPA of 2.0
  - Settle all outstanding fees
  - Submit graduation application by the announced deadline

**IX. Complaints & Appeals**

* Students may submit complaints related to:
  - Academic assessment
  - Administrative decisions
  - Student services
* All complaints must be submitted in writing and will be reviewed within 10 working days.
